title: Orchestrated build — six models build one real app on disk

segments.subtitle: agent-garrison · the orchestrator routes each build step to a fit-for-purpose runtime — Sonnet · Ollama · Opus · GPT (Codex) · Gemini — and the real files land on disk · 2026-06-17

beats.caption: The orchestrator's routing policy (build profile) — each task-type maps to a fit-for-purpose runtime/model
beats.expectedScreen: the Model Router 'Role → target (build)' table: standard→cc-sonnet-med, fast→sdk-ollama-build, expert→cc-opus-high, review→sec-codex, image→sec-gemini, video→cc-opus-high

beats.caption: Step 1 — data model → Claude Sonnet writes src/model.mjs
beats.expectedScreen: the chat showing Sonnet created src/model.mjs, status strip runtime: claude-code · model: sonnet

actions.text: Build the data model for our task CLI in /Users/ggomes/garrison-demo/taskline: create the file src/model.mjs (absolute: /Users/ggomes/garrison-demo/taskline/src/model.mjs) that exports makeTask({title}) returning an object {id, title, done:false, createdAt} where createdAt is an ISO timestamp and id comes from newId() imported from ./id.mjs. Use the Write tool to create the real file. Keep it about 10 lines.

beats.caption: Step 2 — id helper → local Ollama (qwen2.5-coder) generates it; the orchestrator commits it verbatim
beats.expectedScreen: the chat showing the generated newId() helper code and status strip runtime: agent-sdk · provider: ollama-local · model: qwen2.5-coder, plus a 'committed verbatim to src/id.mjs' note

actions.text: Write the JavaScript for the helper file src/id.mjs: a function newId() that returns a short unique id string (base36 timestamp plus a few random base36 chars). Reply with only the code.

beats.caption: Step 3 — the core TaskStore → Claude Opus writes src/store.mjs (the hard build task)
beats.expectedScreen: the chat showing Opus created src/store.mjs with persistence, status strip model: opus

actions.text: Implement the core algorithm: with the Write tool create /Users/ggomes/garrison-demo/taskline/src/store.mjs — a TaskStore class that persists tasks to data/tasks.json (resolve the path via import.meta.url). Methods: add(title) using makeTask from ./model.mjs, list(), complete(id), remove(id). Load in the constructor and save after each change. Do NOT add missing-file handling yet.

beats.caption: Step 4 — code review → GPT via the Codex runtime reads the real file and finds the first-run crash
beats.expectedScreen: the chat showing GPT's review findings about ENOENT on first run with line references, status strip runtime: codex · provider: openai · model: gpt-5-codex

actions.text: Review the file src/store.mjs in this project for correctness bugs. Read the actual file. What happens on the very first run when data/tasks.json does not exist yet? List the 1-2 most important concrete issues with line references, briefly.

beats.caption: Step 5 — apply the fix → Gemini edits src/store.mjs in place (resilient first-run loading)
beats.expectedScreen: the chat showing Gemini applied the fix to src/store.mjs, status strip runtime: gemini · provider: google · model: gemini-2.5-flash

actions.text: Apply the code-review fix to the file src/store.mjs in this project: make loading resilient when data/tasks.json is missing or empty — it must start with an empty task list instead of throwing (handle the missing-file and empty/invalid-JSON cases). Edit the actual file in place and save it. Keep the change minimal.

beats.caption: Step 6 — tests + CLI → Claude Opus (back to the orchestrator) writes test/store.test.mjs and src/cli.mjs
beats.expectedScreen: the chat showing Opus created the test file and the position-based CLI, status strip model: opus

actions.text: Write tests and a CLI with the Write tool. Create /Users/ggomes/garrison-demo/taskline/test/store.test.mjs (node:test, ~5 cases: add, list, complete, remove, persistence — use a temp data file). Create /Users/ggomes/garrison-demo/taskline/src/cli.mjs with commands: add <title>, list, done <n>, remove <n>. <n> is the 1-based list position (not the id); list prints numbered tasks 1,2,3.

segments.caption: The real artifact runs — add tasks, complete one by position, and the JSON persists on disk
segments.expectedScreen: a terminal running node src/cli.mjs add/list/done 1/list against the freshly built app, then cat data/tasks.json showing the persisted tasks
commands: node src/cli.mjs add 'Ship the cross-model build'

segments.caption: The orchestrator's decision log — which model built each file
segments.expectedScreen: terminal listing the 6 build steps: data model → cc-sonnet-med (sonnet); id helper → sdk-ollama-build (agent-sdk/qwen2.5-coder); core store → cc-opus-high (opus); review → sec-codex (codex/gpt-5-codex); apply fix → sec-gemini (gemini); tests+CLI → cc-opus-high (opus)

segments.text: One orchestrator · six models · one working app
segments.subtitle: model→Sonnet · helper→Ollama (local) · core→Opus · review→GPT (Codex) · fix→Gemini · tests→Opus — every file built on disk, the CLI runs, 5/5 tests pass
